english: *i man* (i man) this it is not; *mán-na* (man na) is it not so? isn't it? is it? In conjunction with a negative it is col. almost the only word for only, but etc.: *de mán-na mi yoṅ* (de man na mi yong), *de mán-na med* (de man na med) only this one is to be met with, besides this there are none; *la-dág-gi lug čuṅ-se mán-na mi yoṅ* (la dwags gi lug chung se man na mi yong) there are only small sheep in Ladak; *dúṅ-la mán-na mi tóṅ-k̇an* (drung la man na mi mthong mkhan) he who sees only what is close before him, a short-sighted person; *ḍé-bu lo gyad tiṅ-la mán-na mi yoṅ* ('bras bu lo brgyad rting la man na mi yong) fruit will appear only after a space of eight years; *ḍi-riṅ mán-na ma toṅ* (de ring man na ma mthong) I have seen (him, it) only to-day, i.e. to-day for the first time cf. min (min). — 2. no. — III. = ma (ma) II., man-yáṅ (man yan) below and above Cs.; man-čád (man chad), -čád (chad), -čód (chod) 1. adv. and postp.c.accus., below, downward, on the lower side of, as far as, lté-ba man-čád ču náṅ-du nub (lte ba man chad chu nang du nub) Glr., he was immerged in the water below his navel, i.e. up to his navel; inst. of man-čád (man chad) also merely man (man): pús-mo goṅ man (pus mo gong man) Mil., lit below the parts over the knee i.e. higher than the knee; de man-čód (de man chod), below that Glr.; in reference to time, from, do-núb man čad (do nub man chad) from this evening Mil.; de man-čád (de man chad) since, from that time forward Mil.; rmaṅ btiṅ-ba man rab-gnás mdzád-pa yáṅ-la (rmang bting ba man rab gnas mdzad pa yan la) from the foundation up to the consecration Glr.; even to (the last man), (all) except or save (one), also *mán-pa* (man pa), *mán-pe* (man pe), *mán-k̇an* (man mkhan), *man-na* (man na) W. (B. min-pa (min pa)). — 2. sbst. lower part of a country, lowland, thus in Lh. as a proper name.

Ssk. उपदेश, advice, direction, information, stón-pa (ston pa) to give, man-ṅag (-gi)-rgyúd (man ngag (-gi) rgyud) v. rgyud (rgyud) 2; in later writings and in the mind of the common people, it coincides with sṅags (sngags), in as much as the esoteric doctrines of mysticism, i.e. magic art, are concerned, which are communicated in no other way than by word of mouth; cf. k̇a-rgyan (kha rgyan).

wylie: man chad, chad, chod, pa, lhag

english: मन्दारव, a tree in paradise Stg.
Ld., turbid, muddy, dingy, dim, dull, dusky, as to water, flames of light etc.
1. Sch. 'a small square table', acc. to others a tripod with long curved feet, for sacrificial purposes. — 2. W. bed Hindi मञ्च.

I. sbst., resp. gsol-már (gsol mar) 1. butter Thgy, C., W. — 2. col. also oil. — Comp. skya-már (skya mar), Ld. k̇ág-la mar (khag la mar) fresh, not melted butter; ba-már (ba mar) cow-butter; °bri-már ('bri mar) yak-butter; °bru-már ('bru mar) oil from oleaginous seeds, rape-seed oil etc. Dzl. and elsewhere; rtsi-már (rtsi mar) oil from the stones of apricots etc.; mdzo-már (mdzo mar) butter from the bastard-cow; żun-már (zhun mar) melted butter, ghí (Hind.), the usual form of butter in India and frq. also in Tibet, highly esteemed both as food and as medicine; *żum-már-pa* (zhum mar pa) C. lamp; mar-dkár (mar dkar) Med. = skya-már (skya mar). — már-k̇u (mar khu) melted, liquid butter. — mar-rnyiṅ (mar rnying) old, rancid butter, recommended by physicians for diseases of the mind, fainting-fits, wounds. — *mar-nág* (mar nag) W. oil, *nyuṅ-dkar-mar-nág* (nyung dkar mar nag) rape-seed oil. — *mar-blúg* (mar blug) W. a small urn-shaped vessel for butter or oil. — mar-mé (mar me) lamp, at present only for holy uses, thus: *mar-mé ghyen-tṣéṅ* (mar me gyen tsheng) holy, heavenward burning lamp C. (formerly any lamp Dzl. VS, 11; Glr.); mar me mdzád (mar me mdzad) Buddha Dīpaṅkara, v. Dzl. XXXVII.; — mar-żógs (mar zhogs) Mil. a part cut off, one half of a mar-ril (mar ril), i.e. a globular lump of fresh butter, about one pound in weight, not unfrequently offered to travellers as a gift of courtesy. — mar-gsár (mar gsar) fresh butter Lt. — II. termin. of ma (ma) I., to or 'into' the mother; mar-gyur gró-ba (mar gyur 'gro ba) regarded as a mother, a creature loved like a mother, Mil.; v. ma (ma) II. down, downward, már-la (mar la) id., B. and C.; v. rbab (rbab) and grib-pa (grib pa); mar-ṅo (mar ngo) v. ṅo (ngo) 5.
(?) a fine ochreous earth, found e.g. on the Baralasa pass between Lh. and Ld., used
